Throughout all times, people have valued their own comfort and benefit over what is best for everyone. Even those who claim to follow religion often prioritize themselves instead of Christ’s interests. Few have felt such devotion to sacred things and places that they would give up enjoyment for them. Yet, our souls should certainly desire to live where there are many devout people and opportunities for spiritual growth. If we do not feel this love for the city of God and everything that helps us connect with the Savior, how will we be willing to leave this world; to depart from our bodies so that we may be with the Lord? For those who are focused on worldly things, the perfect holiness of the New Jerusalem would still be difficult to accept than the holiness of God’s church here on earth. Let us seek first God's favor and his glory; let us strive to be patient, content, and helpful in our different roles, and wait with hopeful expectation for entrance into God’s holy city.
